title: China Blocks Meta’s Manus Takeover, Upending a Bet on Singapore’s AI Sanctuary Role
summary: A 54-character directive from Beijing has unwound a $2 billion deal — and with it, the idea that Singapore could serve as a neutral bridge between Chinese AI talent and American capital.

title: OPEC+ Agrees Symbolic Quota Hike as UAE Touts Oil Investment
summary: Major OPEC+ nations agreed a modest and symbolic increase in their June production quota levels, as the group sends a business-as-usual message following the surprise exit of the United Arab Emirates. Abu Dhabi at the same moment touted its own growth plans.

title: Las Vegas Is Gambling on Its Own Reinvention
summary: Tourism is down, lower-income visitors are tightening their wallets, and online gambling means the casino now fits in your pocket. But Las Vegas isn't panicking. It's doubling down on what you can't get on a screen. The Tao Group is building a 46,000-square-foot dayclub on the Strip, its flagship nightclub is posting record revenue year after year, and the city's fast permitting process lets it rebuild itself faster than anywhere else in America. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Traders Grapple With Two-Sided Tail Risk as Stocks Regain Highs
summary: With equities back to their highs after a V-shaped recovery from the Iran oil shock, investors are increasingly caught between left and right tail risks: The tireless AI and semiconductor rally on one side and the gradual drag from higher energy prices on the other.

title: US Navy Taps AI Firm to Clear Iranian Mines in Strait of Hormuz
summary: Traffic through the waterway has been at a near-standstill since the US and Israel launched strikes against Iran in late February and Tehran threatened to attack ships in the strait. Iran has said it laid mines along the most frequently used routes of the narrow waterway, and a senior US Defense Department official says clearing the strait of mines could take six months. Domino Data Lab Chief Operating Officer Thomas Robinson joins David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss the adoption of artificial intelligence (AI) to detect Iranian sea mines in the Strait of Hormuz to speed up and improve the accuracy of underwater mine detection while reducing reliance on human personnel. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Big Tech Earnings Show Split Between AI Trade Winners and Losers
summary: The world’s biggest technology companies posted strong earnings last week, showing that the artificial intelligence boom is alive and well. But in the stock market, investors are getting more granular as they try to divvy up the winners and losers in the AI trade.

title: Sununu: Spirit Bailout "Made No Financial Sense Whatsoever"
summary: The collapse of Spirit Airlines is cascading through airports across the US, leaving passengers stranded mid-journey and sending rival carriers into rapid-response mode to contain the damage. Airlines for America President & CEO Chris Sununu joins David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: The Energy Source Hiding Beneath Your Feet
summary: As data centers push US electricity demand toward a projected 20% increase over the next decade, one overlooked power source is getting a second look: geothermal. New techniques borrowed from oil and gas fracking are unlocking heat from deep underground in places far beyond traditional hot springs. Private capital has poured more than $1.5 billion into next-gen geothermal since 2021, and companies like Ormat and Sage Geosystems are partnering to bring commercial projects online by 2027. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Argentines Keep Cash Under Mattresses Even as Milei Pushes Bank Accounts
summary: You’d be surprised how damp and smelly dollar bills get, Alejandro Lamas says, when they’ve been squirreled away under a mattress for years. He’s gotten so many stacks of them from Argentines he’s sold used cars to over the years that he’s a bit of a connoisseur. Slip him a counterfeit and he knows at first touch.

title: Ukraine Says Russia’s Primorsk Oil Export Port Damaged in Attack
summary: Ukraine said it damaged Russia’s Primorsk port on the Baltic Sea, a key oil export hub, in a drone barrage that also targeted several vessels.

title: Adnoc to Speed Spending Post-OPEC with $55 Billion in Awards
summary: The United Arab Emirates’ flagship oil company Adnoc announced plans to accelerate a growth plan with 200 billion dirham ($55 billion) in project awards spanning upstream and downstream operations, following the country’s exit from OPEC on May 1.

title: Beijing Tells China Firms to Ignore US Sanctions on Refiners
summary: China ordered companies in the country not to comply with US sanctions on five domestic refiners linked to the Iranian oil trade, deploying a blocking measure introduced in 2021 that was aimed at protecting its firms from foreign laws it deemed unjustified.

title: HORMUZ TRACKER: Traffic Halts as Trump Weighs Iran’s New Offer
summary: Traffic through the Strait of Hormuz has come to a virtual standstill while US President Donald Trump weighs Tehran’s latest peace proposal.

title: US Jobs Report to Show Resilience in the Wake of Iran War
summary: The energy shock from the Iran war may be casting a shadow over the outlook for the US economy, but the labor market is yet to register any signs of it.

title: Takaichi Set for Australia Visit to Strengthen Japan Alliance
summary: Japan’s prime minister is set to arrive in Australia to strengthen ties with one of her country’s strongest allies as she seeks to build on an updated regional strategy laid out in Vietnam.

title: OPEC+ Meeting Offers Chance to Show Unity After UAE’s Shock Exit
summary: This weekend’s talks among OPEC+ nations provide the oil producer group with an opportunity to project unity after the shock departure of longtime member the United Arab Emirates.

title: Philippines Accuses China of Conducting Illegal Marine Research
summary: The Philippine Coast Guard said four Chinese vessels were conducting illegal marine scientific research in its waters and threatened to deploy aircraft and boats to repel them.

title: Oil Tanker Pricing Feud Embroils Centuries-Old London Market
summary: A legal claim from one of the world’s largest oil traders is throwing a spotlight on an arcane but critical corner of global finance — the multibillion-dollar freight market and the 282-year-old City of London institution that sits at the heart of it.

title: South African Wine Estates Become Focus for Global Buyers
summary: Global investors are pouring money into commercial wineries in and around Cape Town, even as wine sales slow worldwide. French, German, and Norwegian consortiums are buying into one of the few wine-producing regions where vineyard prices are still climbing. Bloomberg's Jennifer Zabasajja reports. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Golden Tempo Takes Kentucky Derby, Makes History for Female Trainer
summary: LOUISVILLE, Ky. (AP) — After being asked all week about the possibility of becoming the first woman to train a Kentucky Derby winner, Cherie DeVaux was nearly speechless when Golden Tempo charged from the back of the pack Saturday to make history for her.

title: Vietnam Inflation Quickens as Iran War Raises Energy Costs
summary: Vietnam’s inflation picked up more than expected in April, as a surge in global energy prices driven by the Iran war began feeding through to transport and input costs.

title: Philippines Says Thousands Evacuated as Mayon Volcano Erupts
summary: The Philippines said on Sunday that thousands of people have evacuated the area south of Manila after Mayon Volcano erupted, and authorities advised the public to stay out of the six-kilometer radius danger zone.

title: Japan’s Katayama Declines to Comment on FX Intervention For Now
summary: Japanese Finance Minister Satsuki Katayama declined to comment if authorities intervened to support the yen last week, after reports said they had entered the market for the first time since 2024.

title: Aegon, Barclays Say Prepare for Market Pain
summary: Skeptics including Aegon Asset Management and Barclays Plc are getting ready for April’s credit rally to potentially vanish as quickly as it appeared.

title: Spirit Shuts Operations After White House Bailout Collapses
summary: Spirit Aviation Holdings is winding down operations after the troubled US discount carrier buckled under the weight of surging fuel prices and a government bailout dangled by US President Donald Trump fell through. All Spirit flights have been canceled, and passengers have been advised not to go to the airport. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Columbia’s Outlook Revised to Negative as Trump Slams Colleges
summary: Columbia University’s credit outlook was revised to negative by Moody’s Ratings, which cited rising risks tied to the “federal environment for higher education.”

title: Spirit Airlines Shuts Down at Airports Across US
summary: The collapse of Spirit Airlines is cascading through airports across the US, leaving passengers stranded mid-journey and sending rival carriers into rapid-response mode to contain the damage. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Bloomberg This Weekend 5/02/2026
summary: The news doesn’t stop when markets close. Hosts David Gura, Christina Ruffini and Lisa Mateo bring clarity, context and a bit of humor to the weekend’s biggest headlines, LIVE from New York. Joined by The Telegraph Senior Foreign Correspondent Adrian Blomfield, Actor Ben McKenzie, The Points Guy Managing Editor Clint Henderson and GasBuddy Head of Petroleum Analysis Patrick De Haan. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: UAE Departs OPEC as Iran Feel Economic Sting of Blockade
summary: Exxon Mobil Corp. and Chevron Corp. posted stronger-than-expected earnings for the first quarter as higher oil and natural gas prices outweighed production outages from the Iran war. Bloomberg Opinion Energy and Commodities Columnist joins David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Iran War Drags On as Blockade Continues in Hormuz
summary: Telegraph Senior Foreign Correspondent Adrian Blomfield provided an on-the-ground, and on the water, report from the Strait of Hormuz. Blomfield joins David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss how Iran maintains its blockade despite losing much of its naval forces. (Source: Bloomberg)
